title: Drepanocarpus paniculatus
uses: Drepanocarpus paniculatus, a plant native to tropical regions, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are believed to possess antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat skin infections and wounds. The plant is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis. The roots of Drepanocarpus paniculatus are sometimes used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and constipation. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
